publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2021-02-18 18:27:42 -0500.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

origination.label: creator
origination.persname: Custer, Elizabeth Bacon, 1842-1933. (Title of work: Boots and saddles..)

origination.label: creator
origination.persname: Smith, Harlan Ingersoll, 1872-1940. (Title of work: Costumed human figure from Tampico, Washington..)

origination.label: creator
origination.corpname: United States. Bureau of Indian Affairs

origination.label: creator
origination.corpname: Museum of the American Indian, Heye Foundation.

did.abstract: Includes correspondence, newspaper clippings, photographs, notebooks, brochures, manuscripts, maps, paintings, and sketches. Focus areas of the notes for articles, speeches, and books cover a wide variety of topics including the Onondaga Indians and their reservation, festivals and culture, Sitting Bull using a telephone, Tendoi The climber, articles by Elizabeth Custer, Moravian missions, history of Amenia, N.Y., Fort Buford, manuscript regarding Indian attitudes towards African Americans, Low Dog, Jean Francois Millet and his Indian paintings, Stockbridge, Karl Bodmer, Indian costumes, and articles from Century and Antiques Magazine. Correspondence from the United States Indian Service, Luther Kelly, B. M. Graff, Edgar S. Cameron, and the Linnaean Society of New York. Pamphlets include information on saddles, big game hunting, and Iroquois mythology. Maps, from the late 1800s, are annotated by Smith with the paths he traveled by wagon. Maps include Oregon, Indian reservations of the United States, and western and southwestern United States. Oil paintings, charcoal and pencil sketches, of Indians and reservations.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

bioghist.head: BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
bioghist.p: DeCost Smith was born in Skaneateles New York. His devotion to Indian subjects in his artwork gained him appreciation and allowed him to continue to record their lives and customs.

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence, newspaper clippings, photographs, notebooks, brochures, manuscripts, maps, paintings, and sketches. Focus areas of the notes for articles, speeches, and books cover a wide variety of topics including the Onondaga Indians and their reservation, festivals and culture, Sitting Bull using a telephone, Tendoi The climber, articles by Elizabeth Custer, Moravian missions, history of Amenia, N.Y., Fort Buford, manuscript regarding Indian attitudes towards African Americans, Low Dog, Jean Francois Millet and his Indian paintings, Stockbridge, Karl Bodmer, Indian costumes, and articles from Century and Antiques Magazine. Correspondence from the United States Indian Service, Luther Kelly, B. M. Graff, Edgar S. Cameron, and the Linnaean Society of New York. Pamphlets include information on saddles, big game hunting, and Iroquois mythology. Maps, from the late 1800s, are annotated by Smith with the paths he traveled by wagon. Maps include Oregon, Indian reservations of the United States, and western and southwestern United States. Oil paintings, charcoal and pencil sketches, of Indians and reservations.

arrangement.head: COLLECTION ARRANGEMENT
arrangement.p: Original Huntington Free Library box numbers have been retained.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: De Cost Smith Collection #9174. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Objects associated with this collection are at the National Museum of the American Indian.

Indians of North America -- Tobacco use.

Cree Indians.
Crow Indians.
Dakota Indians.

Omaha Indians.

Siksika Indians.
Stockbridge Indians.
Wyandot Indians.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence with between Smith and Graff regarding the artist J. F. Millet. Also photos and newspaper clippings.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence regarding a coat owned by Smith that may have been drawn by Sitting Bull. Some correspondence with Joshua Low Dog. Also photos.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence, photographs, printed material, notes, regarding Millet and his paintings. Includes excepts from the Century Magazine, May 1910, a copy of The Month at Goodspeed's, March-April, 1945.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Titles include: L.D. Stone and Co catalog, A Big Game Paradise, Huron Folk-Lore, A Costumed Human Figure from Tampico Washington, Wanderings in South America.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Maps of the United States and Indian Territory, some annotated.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Sketchbook includes drawings of the Onondaga by Smith and Tom Beaver.
